name: Databricks Observability - Jobs & DLT (Generic)
description: Generic Lakeview dashboard template for monitoring Databricks Jobs and Delta Live Tables (pipelines): runs, failures, and DBU usage/cost placeholders. You MUST edit the SQL queries to match your workspace's system table schema/table names.

datasets.name: ds_job_runs_daily
datasets.query:
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_runs with your workspace system table.
-- Expected columns (generic): workspace_id, job_id, job_name, run_id, run_start_time, run_end_time, result_state, run_duration_ms
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('day', run_start_time) AS day,
    workspace_id,
    job_id,
    job_name,
    run_id,
    result_state,
    run_duration_ms
  FROM system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_runs
  WHERE run_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND run_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
    AND (('{{p_job_name}}' = '') OR lower(job_name) LIKE concat('%', lower('{{p_job_name}}'), '%'))
)
SELECT
  day,
  workspace_id,
  job_id,
  job_name,
  COUNT(*) AS runs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN result_state = 'SUCCESS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS success_runs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN result_state IN ('FAILED', 'TIMEDOUT') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_runs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN result_state IN ('CANCELED', 'CANCELLED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS canceled_runs,
  AVG(run_duration_ms)/1000.0 AS avg_duration_s,
  percentile_approx(run_duration_ms/1000.0, 0.95) AS p95_duration_s
FROM base
GROUP BY 1,2,3,4
ORDER BY day ASC

datasets.name: ds_job_failures
datasets.query:
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_run_tasks (or equivalent) with your workspace system table.
-- Expected columns (generic): run_start_time, workspace_id, job_id, job_name, run_id, task_key, result_state, termination_code, error_message
SELECT
  run_start_time,
  date_trunc('day', run_start_time) AS day,
  workspace_id,
  job_id,
  job_name,
  run_id,
  task_key,
  result_state,
  termination_code,
  error_message
FROM system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_run_tasks
WHERE run_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND run_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND result_state IN ('FAILED', 'TIMEDOUT')
  AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
  AND (('{{p_job_name}}' = '') OR lower(job_name) LIKE concat('%', lower('{{p_job_name}}'), '%'))
ORDER BY run_start_time DESC

datasets.name: ds_dbu_usage_daily
datasets.query:
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.billing_usage (or equivalent) with your workspace system table.
-- Expected columns (generic): usage_start_time, workspace_id, cluster_id, dbu
SELECT
  date_trunc('day', usage_start_time) AS day,
  workspace_id,
  cluster_id,
  SUM(dbu) AS dbu
FROM system.<SCHEMA>.billing_usage
WHERE usage_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND usage_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
GROUP BY 1,2,3
ORDER BY day ASC

datasets.name: ds_job_cost_daily
datasets.query:
-- Generic attribution: joins job runs -> cluster_id -> daily DBU usage.
-- This is approximate (best when jobs use job clusters; shared clusters will be noisy).
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_runs and system.<SCHEMA>.billing_usage with your system tables.
WITH runs AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('day', run_start_time) AS day,
    workspace_id,
    job_id,
    job_name,
    cluster_id
  FROM system.<SCHEMA>.jobs_job_runs
  WHERE run_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND run_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
    AND (('{{p_job_name}}' = '') OR lower(job_name) LIKE concat('%', lower('{{p_job_name}}'), '%'))
), usage AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('day', usage_start_time) AS day,
    workspace_id,
    cluster_id,
    SUM(dbu) AS dbu
  FROM system.<SCHEMA>.billing_usage
  WHERE usage_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND usage_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
    AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
  GROUP BY 1,2,3
)
SELECT
  r.day,
  r.workspace_id,
  r.job_id,
  r.job_name,
  SUM(u.dbu) AS dbu_attributed,
  (SUM(u.dbu) * CAST('{{p_dbu_to_usd}}' AS DOUBLE)) AS usd_attributed
FROM runs r
JOIN usage u
  ON u.workspace_id = r.workspace_id
 AND u.cluster_id = r.cluster_id
 AND u.day = r.day
GROUP BY 1,2,3,4
ORDER BY day ASC

datasets.name: ds_dlt_updates_daily
datasets.query:
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.dlt_pipeline_updates with your workspace system table.
-- Expected columns (generic): update_start_time, workspace_id, pipeline_id, pipeline_name, update_id, update_state
SELECT
  date_trunc('day', update_start_time) AS day,
  workspace_id,
  pipeline_id,
  pipeline_name,
  COUNT(*) AS updates,
  SUM(CASE WHEN update_state IN ('COMPLETED', 'SUCCESS') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed,
  SUM(CASE WHEN update_state IN ('FAILED', 'CANCELED', 'CANCELLED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_or_canceled
FROM system.<SCHEMA>.dlt_pipeline_updates
WHERE update_start_time >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND update_start_time <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
  AND (('{{p_pipeline_name}}' = '') OR lower(pipeline_name) LIKE concat('%', lower('{{p_pipeline_name}}'), '%'))
GROUP BY 1,2,3,4
ORDER BY day ASC

datasets.name: ds_dlt_failures
datasets.query:
-- TODO: Replace system.<SCHEMA>.dlt_pipeline_events with your workspace system table.
-- Expected columns (generic): workspace_id, pipeline_id, pipeline_name, update_id, event_timestamp, event_type, message
SELECT
  workspace_id,
  pipeline_id,
  pipeline_name,
  update_id,
  event_timestamp,
  event_type,
  message
FROM system.<SCHEMA>.dlt_pipeline_events
WHERE event_timestamp >= CAST('{{p_date_start}}' AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND event_timestamp <  CAST(date_add('{{p_date_end}}', 1) AS TIMESTAMP)
  AND (('{{p_workspace_id}}' = '') OR CAST(workspace_id AS STRING) = '{{p_workspace_id}}')
  AND (('{{p_pipeline_name}}' = '') OR lower(pipeline_name) LIKE concat('%', lower('{{p_pipeline_name}}'), '%'))
  AND (
    lower(event_type) LIKE '%error%'
    OR lower(message) LIKE '%error%'
    OR lower(event_type) LIKE '%fail%'
    OR lower(message) LIKE '%fail%'
  )
ORDER BY event_timestamp DESC

widgets.title: Job Failure Rate
widgets.value: CASE WHEN SUM(runs) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (SUM(failed_runs) / SUM(runs)) END

widgets.title: Pipeline Failure Rate
widgets.value: CASE WHEN SUM(updates) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (SUM(failed_or_canceled) / SUM(updates)) END

notes: IMPORT/USE: In Databricks, go to Dashboards (Lakeview) > Import and select this JSON file (or paste).
REQUIRED EDIT: Replace every occurrence of system.<SCHEMA>.* with the correct system schema in your workspace.
If you want a working (non-template) JSON for your workspace, run: SHOW SCHEMAS IN system; and SHOW TABLES IN system.<schema>; and paste results. I will regenerate the JSON with exact table names.
